msgs.text:
Turn 61 — manuscript appendix as a certificate contract, not another sweep I re-read only the three files attached to this turn. The problem is still exactly the over-G isomorphism question for the quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from F=P⋊G and the relators rS,rH. The attached problem statement asks whether there is a continuous isomorphism ΓS→ΓH commuting with the canonical projections to G.

The state at the end of Turn 60 is:ΓS≅GΓH.More strongly, the sweep supports a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup, this descends to the desired over-G quotient isomorphism. Turn 60 also records that no structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached proof; the remaining issue is standalone reproducibility, namely appending verifier scripts or explicit matrices for the finite certificate blocks, especially the D.3 stack-rank 60→61.So Turn 61 should not reopen a new Magnus layer or hammer a new exceptional case. The useful next step is to turn the proof into a certificate contract: a finite list of verifier modules whose outputs imply the theorem.

Certificate contract for the final appendix The appendix should be organized around the following statement.Certificate Theorem. Suppose the following five verifier blocks are supplied.H1 -Nielsen block.Verify that, with the fixed orientationρS:=rS−1,the linearized relator rows satisfywS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E),and that there is a matrix

U∈GL2 (Λ),Λ=Z2 [[G]],carrying wS to wH. On the dangerous Ef-piece, this includes the explicit matrixU0=( s−11 s−22),(s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),with determinant s, a unit. This fixes the first Magnus layer of the filtered Nielsen transformation.Primitive-boundary block.Construct the primitive spacesLn=PrimnF2⟨A,B⟩by the reduced-coproduct method. Then verify the boundary ranks for∂d:Ld⊕Ld→Ld+1,(u,v)↦[A,u]+[B,v].

The already recorded verifier output includesdimL1,…,dimL10=2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36,56,105,and the tableddimLd rank∂d dimker∂d466656936111847183068365616 with the corresponding secondary quotient ranks continuing through d=10. Turn 52 records that this linear verifier was implemented and reproduced the primitive dimensions, boundary ranks, and secondary quotient ranks.Residual restricted-Lie induction block.Verify the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 induction. In the residual equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q=X2,the first nonzero error is primitive, and the correction boundary is the same∂n−1:Ln−1⊕Ln−1→Ln.

The verifier/proof must certify the recursionLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even,together with the inductionCm=Lm (m≥5).This is the uniform statement that every odd residual layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W↦W[2]. The attached history records this as the closure of the whole residual tower.Coefficient Koszul–Witt block.Work on the local Ef-coefficient ring

R=EfΛ≅Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

First normalize the row exactly:(s2+1,s−1)∼(2,t),using(s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2.

Thus the raw term 2t+t2 is a Nielsen-coordinate artifact, not an obstruction. In the associated graded ringgr(2,t)

R≅F2 [α,β],the row is the regular Koszul row (α,β). The coefficient proof then consists of the first t-layer contractibility, the first 2-Bockstein secondary surjectivityBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4),and the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade. The attached history emphasizes that actual pro-2 representatives produce Witt cascades, not a naïve single Frobenius square, and that ordering by Magnus root degree makes the induction triangular at each finite level.Nonlinear certificate block D.1–D.4.The finite exceptional certificates should be listed as follows:

BlockD.1D.2D.3D.4

Content ker∂8↠L5[2]residual degree-16 λ-toggleA2=R/(4,t),d=4,μ4 -toggled=8 quotient witness

Certificate six independent Bockstein shadowsY0↦Y0Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4)))rankD5,6,7=60, rank(μ4B4D5,6,7)=61θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]The history records that D.3, the last executable certificate, is closed by the stack-rank jump 60→61, and that D.4 kills the residual d=8 codimension-one miss after quotienting by imθ80.If these five blocks are included, the proof is no longer a transcript-dependent sweep. It becomes a checkable finite-certificate proof.

Compactness descent, in final form The final descent should use filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary finite quotient automorphisms.

For a degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, setPN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM),M=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩.

Then P/PN,J is finite, and the PN,J are separated. Define SN,J to be the finite set of tuples(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4 with first Magnus layer UmodJ, satisfying the finite cocycle relation for τσ=τ2, and sendingρS=rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J. The residual induction, coefficient Koszul–Witt induction, and D.1–D.4 certificates prove SN,J=∅ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation. Each finite datum gives an actual finite over-G automorphism because the first layer is invertible, so the finite Frattini criterion applies to P/PN,J.

The sets SN,J are finite and functorial under refinement. Compactness givesN,JlimSN,J=∅.A compatible inverse-limit point gives a continuous filtered Nielsen automorphism α:P→P and a continuous cocycle c:G→P. Together they define Φ(pg)=α(p)c(g)g,an over-G automorphism of F=P⋊G. At every finite level,Φ(ρS)≡rH (modPN,J),so, after intersecting over all (N,J),Φ(rS−1)=rH.Thus Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and Φ descends toΓS≅GΓH.What Turn 61 adds

Turn 61 converts the previous “final proof package” into a precise appendix contract. The key point is uniform: no isolated obstruction layer remains worth hammering. The proof rests on a finite list of certificate modules whose outputs imply nonemptiness of every finite filtered Nielsen solution set, and compactness then gives the global over-G automorphism.

ESTABLISHED: The final standalone proof can be written as a certificate theorem: H1 -Nielsen matrix, primitive-boundary verifier, residual restricted-Lie induction, coefficient Koszul–Witt induction, nonlinear certificates D.1–D.4, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness. These imply a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: the standalone appendix should include the verifier code or explicit matrices/pivots supporting the recorded finite certificate outputs, especially D.3’s rank jump 60→61.

NEXT: Draft the final manuscript appendix in verifier-contract form: inputs, deterministic bases, matrices, expected ranks, nonlinear certificate outputs, and the compactness descent.[TURN61-CERTIFICATE-CONTRACT-FINAL-APPENDIX]
